 {"id":"frankenredis-5l2","title":"Wire CONFIG SET repl-backlog-size and repl-timeout to replication subsystem","description":"## Problem

Two replication constants are hardcoded:

1. fr-runtime/src/lib.rs line 53: `const DEFAULT_REPL_BACKLOG_SIZE: u64 = 1_048_576` (1 MiB)
   - Used at line 778 to compute the backlog sliding window
   - Redis default is also 1 MiB but allows CONFIG SET repl-backlog-size

2. fr-server/src/main.rs lines 40-41:
   - REPLICA_ACK_INTERVAL_MS = 1000 (1s) — Redis uses repl-ping-replica-period (default 10s)
   - REPLICA_RECONNECT_BACKOFF_MS = 250 — not configurable in Redis either but should be reasonable

3. Replication handshake timeouts (line 993-994): 50ms read / 500ms write
   - Redis uses repl-timeout (default 60 seconds)
   - These aggressive values cause spurious failures on loaded networks

## What Redis Does

- CONFIG SET repl-backlog-size dynamically resizes the replication backlog
- CONFIG SET repl-timeout sets the replication stream timeout
- CONFIG SET repl-ping-replica-period sets the REPLCONF ACK interval

## Implementation Plan

1. Move repl_backlog_size to ServerState (mutable)
2. Handle CONFIG SET for repl-backlog-size, repl-timeout, repl-ping-replica-period
3. In fr-server, read timeouts from runtime instead of consts
4. Update INFO replication section to report actual values

## Files to Modify

- `crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs`: ServerState fields, CONFIG SET handler
- `crates/fr-server/src/main.rs`: Replace consts with runtime reads
- `crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs`: INFO replication section","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:03.536367224Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:57:28.437166188Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:57:28.436069031Z","close_reason":"repl-backlog-size and repl-timeout wired to ServerState with CONFIG SET/GET support","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-5l2","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-kgn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:16.128244996Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
